serviceNotes.engine oil: Škoda EU service schedules mirror VW Group fixed inspection intervals: engine oil and filter at 15,000 km or annually (whichever first) on Inspection Service / time-and-distance plans in the owner’s service programme.
serviceNotes.cabin filter: Dust and pollen filter replacement is part of the manufacturer’s periodic maintenance (typically every second fixed inspection, around 30,000 km).
serviceNotes.air filter: Air filter change is specified at the extended maintenance interval in Octavia III booklets (60,000 km is typical for petrol; diesels may differ).
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Petrol TSI models specify spark plug replacement per the maintenance table—60,000 km is a common EU interval for four-cylinder engines.
serviceNotes.fuel filter: TDI models require fuel filter replacement on the long-interval maintenance items; Škoda/VW diesel schedules commonly use 60,000 km—omit on pure petrol cars.
serviceNotes.DSG gearbox fluid: Specialists recommend DSG fluid and filter around 60,000 km for long-term reliability of the dual-clutch unit.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Cooling system protection fades with age; exchange around 120,000 km is a sensible preventive step.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Škoda/VW Group specifies brake fluid replacement every 2 years on EU fixed-interval schedules, approximately every 60,000 km.
serviceNotes.check brake pads: Brake pad inspection is part of the Škoda periodic maintenance programme at each major service.
